marketPulseSubtext: Oil up, yields firm, stocks lower, safety trades back
topDriverSubtext: Oil remains the spark, but pressure is spreading across rates, FX, gold, and volatility
disclaimer: Prototype disclaimer: This screen reflects a manual March 27, 2026 market snapshot, but it is not validated for trading, investment, or risk decisions. It would need to be hooked up to real-time market and news sources, then scored by tested algorithms and monitoring before production use.
rateCurveRead: As of the March 27 snapshot, Treasury yields remained elevated, reinforcing that the market is treating the energy shock as an inflation problem rather than a clean flight-to-safety bond bid.

crossMarket.driver: Broad de-risking and tighter financial conditions
Growth hit by yields, war risk, and megacap weakness
Safe-haven demand and firmer Fed path
Cuts priced out, hike chatter back
Inflation repricing and weak bond demand
Safety bid returns as geopolitical stress rises

drivers.name: Correction broadens beyond tech
drivers.detail: The Dow confirmed a correction and the broader market remains under pressure, signaling that this is no longer just a megacap tech wobble.

drivers.name: Oil shock still leads
drivers.detail: Brent and WTI surged again as the Strait of Hormuz disruption continued to dominate inflation and growth expectations.

drivers.name: Bond market not acting like a classic haven
drivers.detail: Treasury yields stayed elevated instead of collapsing, which suggests inflation repricing and weak auction demand are overpowering the usual flight-to-safety bid.

drivers.name: Safety flow rotates into gold and dollars
drivers.detail: Gold rebounded sharply and the dollar strengthened, showing the defensive trade is spreading across more than just crude and defensive equities.

drivers.name: Volatility and consumer stress are rising
drivers.detail: The VIX moved above 31 while consumer sentiment fell to a three-month low, adding macro stress beyond the market tape itself.

risks.title: Rates shock on top of energy shock
risks.desc: If yields keep rising while oil remains elevated, equity multiples and credit conditions can tighten further.

risks.title: Volatility regime shift
risks.desc: A VIX above 30 suggests the market is vulnerable to bigger intraday swings and sharper headline reactions.

risks.title: Consumer demand rollover
risks.desc: Higher gasoline prices and weaker sentiment could start showing up in discretionary spending and travel-sensitive names.

risks.title: FX and global funding stress
risks.desc: A stronger dollar can tighten conditions globally, pressure weaker currencies, and amplify cross-asset stress.

timeline.headline: Markets stayed defensive as the Hormuz disruption and war headlines remained unresolved.
timeline.effect: Oil held its premium and safety trades stayed active.

timeline.headline: The dollar firmed while Treasury yields stayed elevated rather than collapsing.
timeline.effect: The market reinforced the inflation-shock interpretation rather than a simple safe-haven bond rally.

timeline.headline: U.S. stocks sold off again and the Dow confirmed correction territory.
timeline.effect: Risk-off broadened beyond growth into the wider market.

timeline.headline: Gold rallied sharply and volatility stayed elevated into the weekend.
timeline.effect: Defensive positioning broadened across assets beyond crude alone.

strategies.title: Watch Rates With Oil
strategies.body: The cleanest confirmation of a deeper selloff is not just higher crude, but higher crude alongside firm or rising Treasury yields.
strategies.trigger: WTI near $100 and 10Y stays above roughly 4.4%

strategies.title: Monitor Volatility Regime
strategies.body: A VIX above 30 raises the odds of oversized moves, failed rallies, and fast reversals on every geopolitical headline.

strategies.title: Respect Gold and Dollar Strength
strategies.body: If both gold and the dollar continue to firm together, it would reinforce the idea that markets are still migrating toward protection.
strategies.trigger: Gold holds gains and DXY remains firm

strategies.title: Watch Consumer-Sensitive Segments
strategies.body: Travel, discretionary, and rate-sensitive growth could remain under pressure if gasoline prices and inflation expectations keep rising.
strategies.trigger: Sentiment weakens further and discretionary underperforms
